description: Ground-up redesign of OpenTabs as an open plugin ecosystem. AI agents interact with web applications through authenticated browser sessions. Every web service is a plugin — official and community plugins are distributed via npm, dynamically loaded at runtime, and hot-reloaded without restarting the MCP server. Existing code in __next__/ is reference material only — all code should be written fresh.

userStories.title: Set up __next__/ monorepo workspace structure
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want the __next__/ directory set up as a clean bun monorepo with workspaces for all platform packages, shared TypeScript configs, and correct project references so all packages can build and type-check together. The first step is to clean out all existing source code in __next__/ (platform/, plugins/, root configs) while preserving node_modules for faster installs. Then scaffold fresh. The plugins/ directory is NOT a workspace member.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove existing __next__/platform/ and __next__/plugins/ source directories (rm -rf platform/ plugins/), preserving __next__/node_modules/
Remove existing root config files (__next__/tsconfig.json, __next__/tsconfig.base.json, __next__/package.json) to start clean
Remove __next__/bun.lock so dependencies are resolved fresh against the new package.json
__next__/package.json has bun workspaces pointing to platform/*
__next__/tsconfig.base.json has shared compiler options: strict, ES2022 target, ESNext modules, composite
__next__/tsconfig.json has project references to all platform packages
__next__/.gitignore excludes node_modules, dist, *.tsbuildinfo
platform/ has subdirectories: core, plugin-sdk, plugin-loader, plugin-test-utils, create-plugin, mcp-server, browser-extension
Each platform package has package.json with name (@opentabs/<name>), type: module
Each platform package has tsconfig.json extending ../tsconfig.base.json
plugins/ directory exists with slack/ subdirectory (NOT a workspace member)
bun install succeeds from __next__/ root
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement @opentabs/core — JSON-RPC, messaging, and service types with dynamic registry
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I need the shared types package (@opentabs/core) with JSON-RPC message types, Chrome runtime messaging types, and service definitions including a runtime-mutable service registry with change listeners and derived lookup tables. This package has no external runtime dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/core/src/json-rpc.ts exports JsonRpcRequest, JsonRpcResponse, JsonRpcError types
platform/core/src/messaging.ts exports MessageTypes enum with all Chrome runtime message types (GET_STATUS, STATUS_UPDATE, SIDE_PANEL_OPENED, SIDE_PANEL_CLOSED, CLOSE_SIDE_PANEL, etc.)

platform/core/src/services.ts exports ServiceDefinition interface with type, displayName, environments, domains, urlPatterns, iconName, timeout, defaultUrl, hostPermissions, source, packageName fields
platform/core/src/services.ts exports setServiceRegistry() for initial population, addServiceDefinitions() and removeServiceDefinitions() for runtime mutations
Registry supports change listeners notified on add/remove mutations
Registry derives lookup tables (by type, by URL pattern) recomputed on mutation
platform/core/src/services.ts exports ConnectionStatus and ServiceConnectionStatus types
platform/core/src/index.ts re-exports all types and runtime functions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement @opentabs/core — Plugin manifest, lifecycle hooks, and install payload types
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I need plugin-related type definitions in @opentabs/core: the PluginManifest interface, lifecycle hook types, PluginInstallPayload for extension sync, and JSON-serializable stored variants for chrome.storage. These types are used by the SDK, loader, server, and extension.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/core/src/plugin-manifest.ts exports PluginManifest interface with sections: name, displayName, version, description, author, icon, adapter (domains, urlPatterns, hostPermissions, defaultUrl), service (timeout, environments, authErrorPatterns, healthCheck, notConnectedMessage, tabNotFoundMessage), tools (categories), permissions (network, storage, nativeApis)
Exports StoredPluginManifest — JSON-serializable subset without function references
Exports PluginInstallPayload with name, adapterCode, manifest, serviceDefinitions, serviceConfigs, version, trustTier
Exports StoredServiceDefinition and StoredServiceConfig types

platform/core/src/plugin-lifecycle.ts exports lifecycle hook types: PluginLifecycleContext (base), PluginInstallContext (reason + previousVersion), PluginUninstallContext, PluginEnableContext, PluginDisableContext, PluginSettingsChangeContext (previousSettings + changedKeys)
Exports LifecycleHookName type union and ResolvedPlugin interface
Exports ToolRegistrationFn, McpServerLike, RegisteredToolLike abstract types for SDK/loader use without hard SDK dependency
Exports NativeApiPermission type and TrustTier type ('official' | 'community' | 'local')
All new types re-exported from platform/core/src/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement @opentabs/plugin-sdk — Main entry point with definePlugin and type re-exports
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want the main entry point of @opentabs/plugin-sdk to provide definePlugin() for type-safe manifest definition and re-export all core types I need. The SDK has three entry points: . (main), ./server, and ./adapter. This story covers the main entry.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts exports definePlugin() function that accepts a PluginManifest and returns it (identity with type checking)
Re-exports relevant types from @opentabs/core that plugin authors need (PluginManifest, ServiceDefinition, etc.)
package.json exports map has three entry points: '.' -> src/index.ts, './server' -> src/server.ts, './adapter' -> src/adapter.ts
package.json lists @opentabs/core as a dependency
@opentabs/core is also listed as a peerDependency
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement @opentabs/plugin-sdk — Server entry with tool registrar and request provider
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want the ./server entry of the SDK to provide createToolRegistrar() for registering MCP tools, sendServiceRequest() for routing through the WebSocket relay, sendBrowserRequest() for chrome.* APIs, and runtime permission enforcement. Uses AsyncLocalStorage to track the current tool ID.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-sdk/src/server.ts exports createToolRegistrar(server) that returns { tools: Map, define: function } for registering tools
Exports sendServiceRequest(service, params, action?) that delegates to a pluggable RequestProvider
Exports sendBrowserRequest(action, params?) that is gated by nativeApis permission — rejected at runtime if plugin doesn't declare 'browser' in nativeApis
Exports __setRequestProvider() and __registerPluginPermissions() internal APIs for the MCP server to wire
AsyncLocalStorage tracks current tool ID per request
Exports success(data) and error(err) helpers that format MCP tool results per protocol
Exports extensible error pattern registry for common API errors
RequestProvider interface defined with sendServiceRequest, sendBrowserRequest, reloadExtension methods
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement @opentabs/plugin-sdk — Adapter entry with registerAdapter and helpers
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want the ./adapter entry of the SDK to provide registerAdapter() for MAIN world scripts, JSON-RPC response helpers, and createScopedFetch() for domain-restricted fetching. Adapters run in the web page's JavaScript context.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-sdk/src/adapter.ts exports registerAdapter(name, handleRequest) that registers on window.__openTabs.adapters
Exports ok(id, data) and fail(id, code, message) for JSON-RPC response construction
Exports parseAction(method) that extracts the action portion from method strings
Exports createScopedFetch(allowedDomains, pluginName) for opt-in domain restriction
Exports error code constants (METHOD_NOT_FOUND, INTERNAL_ERROR, etc.)
Adapter code is designed to be built into self-contained IIFEs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement @opentabs/plugin-loader — Manifest Zod schema and validation
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want a well-documented JSON manifest format (opentabs-plugin.json) validated by a Zod schema so I get clear errors when my manifest is invalid. The schema validates structural constraints, cross-field consistency, and security constraints.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-loader/src/manifest-schema.ts exports a Zod schema for opentabs-plugin.json
Schema validates: name format (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens), semver version, Chrome match patterns, environment-to-domain consistency, health check method prefix, network permission coverage
Reserved names (browser, system, extension, plugin, opentabs) are rejected
Overly broad URL patterns (e.g. <all_urls>) and overly broad network permissions are rejected
Exports validatePluginManifest() that returns typed validation result with field paths and actionable error messages
Exports name conflict and URL pattern overlap detection across multiple plugins
$schema field support for IDE autocompletion
platform/plugin-loader/scripts/generate-schema.ts generates JSON Schema from Zod and writes to platform/schemas/plugin-v1.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement @opentabs/plugin-loader — Plugin discovery from node_modules, config, and local paths
userStories.description: As a user, I want plugins discovered automatically from npm installs (naming convention + keyword), explicitly from config files, and from local filesystem paths. Explicit entries take precedence over auto-discovered duplicates.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-loader/src/discover.ts exports discoverPlugins() function
Automatic scan of node_modules for packages matching @opentabs/plugin-* or opentabs-plugin-*
Fallback to opentabs-plugin keyword in package.json for non-standard names
Scoped packages discovered correctly
Plugin lists read from both config files and project-level opentabs.config
Local plugin paths (starting with ./ or ../ or absolute) resolved correctly
Local plugins get trust tier 'local'
Explicit config entries take precedence over auto-discovered duplicates
Returns discovered plugin metadata (package path, source, trust tier)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement @opentabs/plugin-loader — Plugin loading pipeline with dynamic import
userStories.description: As the platform, I need to dynamically import each plugin's tool registration module and adapter IIFE at runtime so plugins work without a platform rebuild. The loader validates manifests, imports tool modules, extracts lifecycle hooks, determines trust tiers, and merges into the service registry.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-loader/src/merge.ts (or similar) exports loadPlugins() that orchestrates the full pipeline: discover -> validate -> load -> merge
Tool modules loaded via dynamic import() with cache-busting query params for hot reload
registerTools export is required from each plugin's tool module; isHealthy and lifecycle hooks are optional
Lifecycle hooks extracted from named exports (onInstall, onUninstall, onEnable, onDisable, onSettingsChange)
Adapter IIFE read from disk and bundled into install payloads for the extension
Trust tiers: @opentabs/plugin-* = official, npm packages with keyword = community, local filesystem = local
Local plugins loaded identically to npm plugins
Failed plugins produce clear error messages and don't crash the platform
Exports LoadPluginsResult type with plugins, failures, registry, toolRegistrations
Exports manifestToServiceDefinition() and manifestToServiceConfigs() converters
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement @opentabs/plugin-test-utils — Test harness and mock provider
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want to unit-test my tool handlers without a running MCP server or browser. The test utils provide a harness that simulates tool registration and invocation, and a mock provider that intercepts service requests.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-test-utils/src/test-harness.ts exports createTestHarness() providing: registerTools(), callTool(name, params), toolNames, getTool(), assertToolRegistered()
callTool() returns ParsedToolResult with .isError, .text, .data, .json<T>() accessors
withToolId context preserved during tool execution
platform/plugin-test-utils/src/mock-provider.ts exports createMockProvider() providing: install(), uninstall(), onServiceRequest() with .resolveWith() / .rejectWith()
Mock provider intercepts sendServiceRequest calls during tests
Both exports available from platform/plugin-test-utils/src/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement create-opentabs-plugin scaffolding CLI
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to run a CLI command to scaffold a new plugin project with all required files, correct structure, and variable substitution so I can start building immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/create-plugin/src/index.ts exports both CLI entry and programmatic scaffoldPlugin(options) API
CLI accepts: plugin name (required), --domain, --display, --description, --author, --output flags
Template includes: opentabs-plugin.json, package.json, tsconfig.json, src/adapter.ts, src/tools/index.ts, src/tools/general.ts
Template variables ({{name}}, {{displayName}}, {{domain}}, etc.) replaced in all generated files
Plugin name validation: lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, not reserved names
Generated package.json has correct peerDependencies (@opentabs/plugin-sdk, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, zod)
Generated package.json has opentabs-plugin keyword and includes opentabs-plugin.json in files array
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement MCP server — Config module and entry point with HTTP/stdio transport
userStories.description: As a user running the MCP server, I want it to start in either HTTP mode (multiple MCP clients via Streamable HTTP + SSE) or stdio mode (single client). The server is the main @opentabs/mcp-server package with config parsing and transport setup.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts exports ServerConfig type and config parsing from env vars / CLI args (httpPort, wsPort, httpHost, mode)
platform/mcp-server/src/index.ts is the entry point — reads config and calls startServer()
platform/mcp-server/src/http-server.ts implements HTTP server with Streamable HTTP transport at /mcp and SSE at /sse
Stdio transport available via StdioServerTransport from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
Each HTTP client session gets its own McpServer instance
Server reads version from package.json
Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
Server logs connection info on startup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement MCP server — WebSocket relay for Chrome extension communication
userStories.description: As the MCP server, I need a WebSocket server that the Chrome extension connects to for bidirectional communication. The relay routes JSON-RPC requests from MCP tool handlers to the extension and returns responses. It must survive hot reloads via globalThis persistence.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/websocket-relay.ts exports a WebSocketRelay class and a module-level relay singleton
relay.start(port) starts a WebSocket server on the given port
relay.sendServiceRequest(service, params, action?) sends JSON-RPC to the extension and returns a Promise of the response
relay.sendBrowserRequest(action, params?) routes to chrome.tabs/windows APIs via the extension
relay.reloadExtension() sends a reload command to the extension
Relay tracks extension connection status (connected/disconnected)
Relay singleton persisted across hot reloads via globalThis
Hot reload updates relay prototype via Object.setPrototypeOf so new/changed methods take effect immediately
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement MCP server — Tool registration pipeline
userStories.description: As the MCP server, I need a central tool registration module that merges platform-native tools (browser, extension) with plugin-provided tools. The pipeline supports dynamic injection of plugin registrations and produces a unified tool map for hot-reload diffing.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/tools/index.ts exports registerAllTools(server) that returns Map<string, RegisteredTool>
PLATFORM_REGISTRATIONS array holds statically imported platform-native tool registration functions
setPluginRegistrations() accepts plugin tool registrations discovered at startup
registerAllTools merges platform + plugin tools with globally unique name enforcement
Tool name collision throws with actionable error message
getToolCountSummary() returns platform, plugin, and total registration counts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement MCP server — Plugin initialization pipeline
userStories.description: As the MCP server, I need to discover, validate, load, and wire all plugins at startup. The plugin-init module bridges @opentabs/plugin-loader with the MCP server's tool pipeline, wires the RequestProvider, registers permissions, builds extension install payloads, and caches resolved plugins for lifecycle hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-init.ts exports initializePlugins() that runs the full discovery/load pipeline
Wires the RequestProvider so plugin tools can call sendServiceRequest()/sendBrowserRequest()
Registers nativeApi permissions for each plugin via __registerPluginPermissions()
Injects plugin tool registrations into tools/index.ts via setPluginRegistrations()
Builds PluginInstallPayload objects (reads adapter IIFE from disk, converts manifest to stored format)
Caches install payloads for re-sending on extension reconnect via getLastPluginPayloads()
Caches resolved plugins for lifecycle hook invocation (invokeOnInstall, invokeOnUninstall, invokeOnEnable, invokeOnDisable, invokeOnSettingsChange)
Returns PluginInitSummary with pluginsLoaded, pluginsFailed, pluginNames, failures
Exports refreshPluginTools() for hot reload (re-discovers without re-freezing registry)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement MCP server — Hot reload system
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the MCP server to hot-reload when compiled files change via bun --hot. The hot reload system diffs old vs new tool definitions, patches existing MCP client sessions in-place, and sends tools/list_changed notifications without disconnecting clients.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/hot-reload.ts exports getHotState() with state persisted across reloads via globalThis
Hot state tracks: initialized flag, reloadCount, lastReload result, active sessions, createServerFn, relay
Exports isHotReload() that returns true when the module is re-evaluated (initialized already true)
Exports registerSession() and closeAllSessions() for session lifecycle
Exports hotPatchAllSessions() that re-registers tools on existing sessions and sends notifications/tools/list_changed
Hot patching diffs old vs new tool maps: adds new tools, removes deleted tools, updates changed tools via RegisteredTool.update()
server.ts handleHotReload() calls wireRequestProvider, refreshPluginTools, and hotPatchAllSessions in sequence
Failed hot reloads are caught and logged — existing sessions keep previous working tools
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement MCP server — Platform-native tools (browser tabs, extension reload, capture)
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need platform-native MCP tools for browser tab management, extension reloading, and page capture. These tools call chrome.* APIs through the WebSocket relay and are always available when the extension is connected.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/tools/browser/tabs.ts exports registerBrowserTabsTools() that registers tab management tools (list tabs, navigate, create tab, close tab, execute script)
platform/mcp-server/src/tools/extension/reload.ts exports registerExtensionReloadTools() that registers reload_extension tool
reload_extension triggers chrome.runtime.reload() via the relay
platform/mcp-server/src/tools/capture/index.ts exports registerCaptureTools() for page capture functionality
All tool registration functions return Map<string, RegisteredTool>
Tools have accurate descriptions for AI agent consumption
Parameter descriptions include examples where helpful
All three registrations added to PLATFORM_REGISTRATIONS in tools/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement MCP server — Health endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer verifying the autonomous development loop, I need a GET /health endpoint that returns JSON with server state, extension connection status, plugin status, and hot reload information.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /health returns JSON with: streamSessions count, sseSessions count
Response includes extension connection status ('connected' or 'disconnected')
Response includes hotReload.reloadCount and hotReload.lastReload (success, timestamp, patchedSessions, toolCount, error)
Response includes plugin information: names, count, failures
Response includes tool count summary (platform, plugin, total)
Endpoint is always available regardless of MCP client connections
Health check added to http-server.ts route handler

userStories.title: Implement ~/.opentabs/ config directory with readConfig and writePluginState
userStories.description: As a user, I want ~/.opentabs/ created automatically on first MCP server run with default config.json and plugins.json. The config module provides typed read/write functions for both files with graceful error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/opentabs-config.ts exports readConfig() that returns typed OpenTabsUserConfig
~/.opentabs/ directory created on first run if it doesn't exist
~/.opentabs/config.json created with default structure: { plugins: [], server: {} }
~/.opentabs/plugins.json created with default structure: {} (empty — all tools disabled by default)
Both files are valid JSON, human-readable, and hand-editable
Exports writePluginState() that writes tool enable/disable changes back to plugins.json
File read errors (permissions, corrupt JSON) are caught and logged — server starts with defaults, not crashes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integrate config system into MCP server startup and hot reload
userStories.description: As a user, I want the MCP server to read ~/.opentabs/config.json at startup and on every hot reload so config changes take effect without a restart. Plugin paths from global config are merged with project-level opentabs.config (project takes precedence).
acceptanceCriteria: initializePlugins() calls readConfig() and uses global plugin paths from ~/.opentabs/config.json as additional discovery sources
refreshPluginTools() re-reads ~/.opentabs/config.json on every hot reload cycle
Plugin paths from global config merged with project-level opentabs.config (project config takes precedence for plugin paths)
Adding a new local plugin path to ~/.opentabs/config.json and triggering a hot reload picks it up
discoverPlugins() already supports opentabs.config files — this story verifies the merge behavior
/health endpoint reports the config file path and last-read timestamp
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement tool enable/disable gating with plugins.json state
userStories.description: As a user, I want all tools of a newly installed plugin to start disabled by default and only appear in the MCP tool list after I explicitly enable them. The plugins.json file supports both individual tool toggles and a plugin-level allToolsEnabled shorthand.
acceptanceCriteria: When initializePlugins() discovers a plugin, it checks ~/.opentabs/plugins.json for per-tool enabled state
If a plugin is not in plugins.json (first discovery), all its tools are treated as disabled
Disabled tools are NOT registered on the MCP server — they don't appear in the tool list
Enabled tools (explicitly set to true in plugins.json) are registered normally
registerAllTools() filters plugin tools based on enabled state before calling server.registerTool()
plugins.json format: { "slack": { "tools": { "slack_send_message": true } } }
Shorthand: { "slack": { "allToolsEnabled": true } } enables all tools for the plugin (individual overrides still apply)
readPluginState() resolves effective enabled state per tool considering both allToolsEnabled and individual overrides
notifications/tools/list_changed sent when tools are enabled/disabled
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement system tool — opentabs_reload_plugins
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need an opentabs_reload_plugins MCP tool that re-discovers plugins and re-reads ~/.opentabs/ config without restarting the server. This is a platform-native system tool, always available.

userStories.title: Implement system tools — opentabs_list_plugins and opentabs_get_health
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need opentabs_list_plugins and opentabs_get_health MCP tools to inspect platform state without opening a browser. Both are platform-native and always available.

opentabs_get_health returns same data as GET /health: server state, extension status, plugin status, hot reload count

userStories.title: Implement system tools — opentabs_get_config and opentabs_set_config
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need opentabs_get_config and opentabs_set_config MCP tools to read and modify non-security server settings. These tools do NOT allow enabling/disabling tools — that security boundary is reserved for the side panel.

opentabs_set_config does NOT allow enabling/disabling tools — returns error if attempted

userStories.title: Implement Chrome extension — Project setup, manifest, and build config

userStories.title: Implement Chrome extension — Offscreen document with persistent WebSocket

userStories.title: Implement Chrome extension — Background script entry and MCP router

userStories.title: Implement Chrome extension — Plugin manager and plugin store
userStories.description: As the extension, I need to dynamically install and uninstall plugins at runtime. The plugin manager receives install payloads from the MCP server and stores manifests, adapter code, and service configs in chrome.storage.local.

userStories.title: Implement Chrome extension — Adapter manager with dynamic content script registration
userStories.description: As the extension, I need to dynamically register and unregister MAIN world content scripts for each installed plugin's adapter. Scripts are injected into tabs matching the plugin's URL patterns.

userStories.title: Implement Chrome extension — Webapp service controller
userStories.description: As the extension, I need a data-driven webapp service controller that handles tool requests for any plugin service. One generic controller is created per plugin — it manages tab detection, health checks, and request routing to the adapter in the target tab.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/browser-extension/src/background/service-controllers/webapp-service-controller.ts is a generic, data-driven controller created from StoredServiceConfig

Service controllers created/destroyed dynamically when plugins are installed/uninstalled

userStories.title: Implement Chrome extension — Browser controller and supporting modules
userStories.description: As the extension, I need the browser controller (native chrome.* API service — no adapters, no tabs, always available) and supporting modules: icon manager, alarm handlers, stale tab manager, content stub, and side panel manager.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/browser-extension/src/background/browser-controller.ts handles browser.* requests by calling chrome.tabs/windows APIs directly

platform/browser-extension/src/background/icon-manager.ts manages extension icon state based on connection status

platform/browser-extension/src/background/side-panel-manager.ts tracks side panel open/close state per window
platform/browser-extension/src/content-stub/index.ts provides a content script stub for chrome API access from web pages
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add WebSocket message types for plugin state management
userStories.description: As the platform, I need the MCP server to expose WebSocket message types that the side panel can use to read plugin/tool state and toggle tool enabled/disabled status. The MCP server is the single source of truth for all settings.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP server handles get_plugin_state WebSocket message — returns all plugins with per-tool enabled/disabled state from ~/.opentabs/plugins.json
MCP server handles set_tool_enabled WebSocket message — toggles a specific tool in plugins.json and triggers notifications/tools/list_changed
Message types defined in @opentabs/core messaging types
Background script relays these messages between side panel (chrome.runtime) and MCP server (WebSocket)
set_tool_enabled writes atomically to plugins.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement side panel — React app with plugin list
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to show all installed plugins with their tools so I can see what's available. The side panel is a React app that reads state from the MCP server (not chrome.storage.local).
acceptanceCriteria: Side panel is a React 19 app in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/
On open, sends get_plugin_state to MCP server via background script
Renders plugin list grouped by name with: display name, version, trust tier badge (official/community/local)
Under each plugin, renders tool names and descriptions
If MCP server is disconnected, shows a 'Not connected' state
Uses Tailwind CSS for styling
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement side panel — Tool enable/disable toggles
userStories.description: As a user, I want to toggle individual tools on/off from the side panel with changes taking effect immediately. Each plugin group has a top-level enable all/disable all toggle.
acceptanceCriteria: Each tool has a toggle switch (enabled/disabled)
Each plugin group has a top-level 'enable all / disable all' toggle
Toggling sends set_tool_enabled to MCP server which writes to plugins.json and triggers notifications/tools/list_changed
Toggle state updates in UI immediately (optimistic update)
Newly installed plugin tools are shown as disabled by default
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement side panel — Tab state indicators
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to show tab state per plugin (closed, not-authed, authed) so I know if I need to open or log in to a web application.
acceptanceCriteria: Each plugin displays a status indicator: green (authed), yellow (not-authed), red (closed)
closed state shows hint: 'Open <url> in your browser'
not-authed state shows hint: 'Log in to <service>'
authed state shows no hint (clean, good state)
Tab state updates received from background script and reflected in real time
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement tool activity event broadcasting from MCP server
userStories.description: As the platform, I need the MCP server to broadcast tool_invocation_start and tool_invocation_end events to the extension so the side panel can show real-time activity. Events are fire-and-forget and emitted at the tool registration wrapper level.
acceptanceCriteria: When a tool handler begins execution, MCP server emits { type: 'tool_invocation_start', plugin, tool, ts } over WebSocket
When a tool handler completes, emits { type: 'tool_invocation_end', plugin, tool, duration_ms, success }
Events are fire-and-forget — server does not wait for acknowledgement
Events emitted for both platform-native tools and plugin tools
Event emission injected at the tool registration wrapper level (not by plugin code)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement side panel — Tool activity animation
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to animate the corresponding plugin/tool entry when a tool is being invoked, showing a visual indicator that clears when the invocation completes.
acceptanceCriteria: Side panel receives tool_invocation_start and tool_invocation_end events from background script
Active tools show a visual indicator (spinner or pulse animation)
Indicator clears when tool_invocation_end is received
Activity is transient — no persistent log
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement console.warn logging on tool invocation in target tab
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every plugin tool invocation to log a console.warn in the target web page's DevTools console with the plugin name, tool name, and a clickable link to the source package.
acceptanceCriteria: When the adapter receives a JSON-RPC request, it logs console.warn in the target tab: '[OpenTabs] <plugin>.<tool> invoked — <link>'
Link is the npm page URL for published plugins, or the local filesystem path for local plugins
The log uses console.warn (not console.log) so it stands out
Logging injected at the adapter dispatch level in adapter-manager.ts — plugin authors do NOT need to add it
Warning is minimal: one line + link, does not log request params or response data
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement outdated plugin check on MCP server startup
userStories.description: As a user, I want the MCP server to check installed npm plugins for newer versions on startup and tell me what bun update command to run. The check is non-blocking and cached in memory.
acceptanceCriteria: On startup, MCP server queries npm registry for each installed npm plugin's latest version (skips local plugins)
Check is non-blocking — server startup does not wait for it
Results logged to CLI stdout with exact bun update command
Results cached in memory and exposed via /health endpoint
Results available to side panel via a WebSocket message type
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement side panel — Outdated plugin notifications
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to show when a plugin has an available update on npm with a badge showing the available version and the bun update command.
acceptanceCriteria: Side panel receives outdated plugin data from MCP server (populated on startup check)
Outdated plugins show a badge with available version and bun update command
Notifications are non-intrusive (small indicator, not a blocking dialog)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Slack plugin — Manifest, adapter, and health check evaluator
userStories.description: As a plugin author (and acceptance tester), I want the Slack plugin to demonstrate the full plugin SDK. The Slack plugin uses @opentabs/plugin-sdk as a peer dependency with zero platform-internal imports. This story covers the manifest, adapter, and health check.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/slack/opentabs-plugin.json manifest covers: adapter config (domains, urlPatterns for app.slack.com), service config with health check (auth.test method), tool categories, permissions
plugins/slack/src/adapter.ts uses registerAdapter from @opentabs/plugin-sdk/adapter to handle 'api' action
Adapter forwards Slack Web API calls using the page's authenticated fetch context
plugins/slack/src/health-check.ts exports a custom isHealthy evaluator for Slack auth.test response
plugins/slack/src/tools/types.ts defines shared types for Slack tool responses
plugins/slack/package.json peer-depends on @opentabs/plugin-sdk, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, zod
No platform-internal imports — only imports from @opentabs/plugin-sdk
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Slack plugin — Messages, search, channels, and conversations tools
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want the Slack plugin's core tool domains implemented: messages (send, list, update, delete), search (messages, files), channels (list, info, create, archive), and conversations (history, replies, mark).
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/slack/src/tools/messages.ts registers message tools using createToolRegistrar from SDK
plugins/slack/src/tools/search.ts registers search tools
plugins/slack/src/tools/channels.ts registers channel management tools
plugins/slack/src/tools/conversations.ts registers conversation tools
All tools use sendServiceRequest() from the SDK to route through the adapter
Error patterns registered for common Slack API errors
plugins/slack/src/tools/index.ts exports registerTools that combines all domain registrations
Tool descriptions are accurate and informative for AI agent use
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Slack plugin — Users, files, pins, stars, and reactions tools
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want the Slack plugin's remaining tool domains implemented: users (list, info, status), files (list, upload, info), pins (add, remove, list), stars (add, remove, list), and reactions (add, remove, get).
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/slack/src/tools/users.ts registers user tools
plugins/slack/src/tools/files.ts registers file tools
plugins/slack/src/tools/pins.ts registers pin tools
plugins/slack/src/tools/stars.ts registers star tools
plugins/slack/src/tools/reactions.ts registers reaction tools
All tools use sendServiceRequest() and follow the same pattern as the core tools
registerTools in index.ts updated to include all domain registrations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify npm distribution readiness for plugins
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want to verify that the package.json structure for both official and community plugins is correct for npm publishing, with proper files, keywords, and peerDependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Slack plugin package.json includes opentabs-plugin.json in files array
Slack plugin package.json keywords includes opentabs-plugin
Slack plugin peer-depends on @opentabs/plugin-sdk, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, zod
Scaffolded plugins from create-opentabs-plugin also have correct files, keywords, and peerDependencies
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: End-to-end plugin flow verification
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want to verify the full plugin lifecycle: scaffold a test plugin, build it, install locally via config path, discover at startup (tools disabled by default), enable a tool, invoke it, hot reload changes, disable tool, uninstall.
acceptanceCriteria: Scaffold a test plugin with create-opentabs-plugin
Build the plugin: bun run build
Install locally via config path in ~/.opentabs/config.json
MCP server discovers the plugin at startup — all tools are disabled by default
Enable a tool from side panel — tool appears in MCP client tool list
Tool invocation works end-to-end (MCP client -> server -> extension -> adapter -> web API)
Tool invocation logs console.warn in the target tab
Side panel animates during tool invocation
Disable tool from side panel — tool removed from MCP client tool list
Code change in plugin -> rebuild -> hot reload picks up changes
bun run build and typecheck pass
Typecheck passes
